programs.programName: Senior Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (Senior SNAP)

verified.notes: Age requirement increased to 66 years or older effective February 2, 2026 (previously 60+). Income limits, asset limits, and specific eligibility thresholds are not provided in available search results. Search results confirm Senior SNAP has income limits but do not specify dollar amounts. Phone number is the DFCS Customer Contact Center main number for Senior SNAP inquiries.

verified.notes: CRITICAL: As of July 1, 2025, the State of Georgia no longer offers SCSEP. The Georgia Department of Human Services discontinued the program. Federal funding was halted in July 2025. Legacy Link and AARP continue to operate SCSEP in some Georgia counties, but the state program is discontinued. Income limit is 125% of federal poverty level (not provided as fixed dollar amounts in sources; varies annually by federal guidelines). No asset limits found in sources. No official Georgia SCSEP phone number available; program no longer state-administered.
